I want to help—what do you mean by “hear the phone”?
- Trouble hearing people during calls?
- Can’t hear the ringtone/alerts?
- Want to use hearing aids or captions?
Also, are you on iPhone or Android (model)?

Quick things to try now:
- During a call: press the side volume up buttons, turn on Speaker, or plug in headphones/earbuds.
- Turn off Bluetooth (audio may be routing to another device).
- Clean the earpiece/speaker grille; remove bulky case/screen protector near the speaker.
- Settings > Sounds: raise call/ring volume, pick a louder ringtone, turn on vibrate.

Helpful accessibility options:
- iPhone: Settings > Accessibility > Audio/Visual: Mono Audio, balance slider, Headphone Accommodations; pair MFi hearing aids; Live Captions (iOS 16+). Control Center > Mic Mode > Voice Isolation can make the other person hear you better.
- Android: Settings > Accessibility: Sound Amplifier, Mono Audio, Hearing aid support; Live Caption; enable RTT/TTY for text during calls.

Captioned calling:
- Enable RTT/TTY (built in) or use a captioned-calls app (e.g., CaptionCall, Hamilton CapTel) if you need on-screen text of what’s said.

Urgent note:
- If you’ve had sudden hearing loss (especially in one ear) within the last 72 hours, new severe ear pain, drainage, or spinning dizziness, seek urgent care now.
